tiers.name: Nonprofits (Free)

dlp.label: Not available
dlp.detail: Content scanning and automated rules require Enterprise

driveLabels.label: Not available
driveLabels.detail: Classification labels require Business Standard or higher

gmailLabels.label: Not available
gmailLabels.detail: Gmail classification labels require Business Standard or higher

domainSharing.label: Available
domainSharing.detail: Can restrict all external sharing at domain level

allowlistedDomains.label: Basic
allowlistedDomains.detail: Can allowlist specific domains for sharing

sharedDriveControls.label: Limited
sharedDriveControls.detail: Cannot restrict sharing per Shared Drive

targetAudiences.label: Not available
targetAudiences.detail: Custom sharing groups require Business Standard

trustRules.label: Not available
trustRules.detail: Granular per-user sharing rules require Enterprise

gmailConfidential.label: Available
gmailConfidential.detail: Set expiration, prevent forwarding/copying. Not true encryption.

irmViewerRestrict.label: Available
irmViewerRestrict.detail: File owners can restrict viewers from downloading/printing/copying

irmAdminEnforced.label: Not available
irmAdminEnforced.detail: Admin-enforced IRM via DLP requires Enterprise

vault.label: Not available
vault.detail: eDiscovery and retention requires Business Plus

recommendations: Set domain-wide sharing to 'Only people in your organization' as default
Use Gmail Confidential Mode for sensitive external emails
Train staff to manually restrict download/print on sensitive files
Use Shared Drive structure with team-based access for basic access control
Enable 2-Step Verification for all users (free and critical)
Enable basic mobile device management (screen lock, remote wipe)
Consider upgrading to Business Standard ($3.50/user/mo for nonprofits) for Drive labels and Shared Drive controls

tiers.name: Business Standard

dlp.label: Not available
dlp.detail: Content scanning and automated DLP rules require Enterprise

driveLabels.label: Available (manual)
driveLabels.detail: Admins can create classification labels; users apply them manually to Drive files

gmailLabels.label: Available (manual)
gmailLabels.detail: Classification labels available for Gmail messages; manual application only

domainSharing.label: Available
domainSharing.detail: Can restrict all external sharing at domain level

allowlistedDomains.label: Available
allowlistedDomains.detail: Can allowlist specific domains for sharing

sharedDriveControls.label: Available
sharedDriveControls.detail: Can restrict sharing settings per individual Shared Drive (prevent external sharing, download, copy)

targetAudiences.label: Available
targetAudiences.detail: Create custom target audiences for sharing suggestions and link default audiences

trustRules.label: Not available
trustRules.detail: Granular per-user or per-OU sharing rules require Enterprise

gmailConfidential.label: Available
gmailConfidential.detail: Set expiration, prevent forwarding/copying. Not true encryption.

irmViewerRestrict.label: Available
irmViewerRestrict.detail: File owners can restrict viewers from downloading/printing/copying

irmAdminEnforced.label: Not available
irmAdminEnforced.detail: Admin-enforced IRM via DLP requires Enterprise

vault.label: Not available
vault.detail: eDiscovery and retention requires Business Plus

recommendations: Create Drive labels matching your classification tiers (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted)
Configure per-Shared Drive sharing restrictions — disable external sharing on drives containing T3/T4 data
Set up target audiences for departments to control sharing suggestions
Use Shared Drive membership as your primary access control mechanism
Train staff to apply classification labels when creating or uploading files
Restrict download/print/copy on Shared Drives containing sensitive data
Continue using Gmail Confidential Mode for sensitive external communications
Enable 2-Step Verification and mobile device management for all users

tiers.name: Business Plus

dlp.label: Not available
dlp.detail: Content scanning and automated DLP rules require Enterprise

driveLabels.label: Available (manual)
driveLabels.detail: Classification labels with manual application to Drive files

gmailLabels.label: Available (manual)
gmailLabels.detail: Classification labels with manual application to Gmail messages

domainSharing.label: Available
domainSharing.detail: Can restrict all external sharing at domain level

allowlistedDomains.label: Available
allowlistedDomains.detail: Can allowlist specific domains for sharing

sharedDriveControls.label: Available
sharedDriveControls.detail: Per-Shared Drive sharing and download restrictions

targetAudiences.label: Available
targetAudiences.detail: Custom target audiences for sharing control

trustRules.label: Not available
trustRules.detail: Granular per-user or per-OU sharing rules require Enterprise

gmailConfidential.label: Available
gmailConfidential.detail: Set expiration, prevent forwarding/copying. Not true encryption.

irmViewerRestrict.label: Available
irmViewerRestrict.detail: File owners can restrict viewers from downloading/printing/copying

irmAdminEnforced.label: Not available
irmAdminEnforced.detail: Admin-enforced IRM via DLP requires Enterprise

vault.label: Available
vault.detail: Google Vault for eDiscovery, retention rules, legal holds, and audit

recommendations: Configure Vault retention rules to match your data retention policy — especially important for T3/T4 data
Set up legal holds for any active litigation or investigation
Use Vault audit reports to monitor who is accessing sensitive data
Create Drive labels matching classification tiers and train all staff on usage
Configure per-Shared Drive restrictions for T3/T4 data stores
Use Vault search and export for compliance reporting and audit requests
Set up target audiences to manage sharing boundaries between departments
Consider Enterprise upgrade for DLP auto-classification if you handle large volumes of T4 data

tiers.name: Enterprise Standard
tiers.nonprofitDiscount: Significant discount available through Google for Nonprofits

dlp.label: Available
dlp.detail: Full DLP: scan content, auto-detect sensitive data (SSN, credit cards, etc.), trigger actions (block sharing, warn users, quarantine)

driveLabels.label: Available (manual + auto)
driveLabels.detail: Manual labels plus DLP-triggered auto-classification based on content scanning

gmailLabels.label: Available (manual + auto)
gmailLabels.detail: Manual labels plus DLP-triggered auto-classification for email content

sharedDriveControls.label: Available
sharedDriveControls.detail: Full per-Shared Drive sharing, download, and access restrictions

targetAudiences.label: Available
targetAudiences.detail: Custom target audiences with advanced controls

trustRules.label: Available
trustRules.detail: Granular trust rules: control sharing per user, OU, or group; restrict by file type, label, or destination

irmViewerRestrict.label: Available
irmViewerRestrict.detail: File owners can restrict viewers from downloading/printing/copying

irmAdminEnforced.label: Available
irmAdminEnforced.detail: Admin can enforce IRM policies via DLP rules — automatically restrict download/print/copy based on content or label

vault.label: Available
vault.detail: Full Google Vault: eDiscovery, retention, legal holds, audit, and export

contextAwareAccess.label: Available
contextAwareAccess.detail: Restrict access based on device security posture, IP address, location, and user identity

securityCenter.label: Available
securityCenter.detail: Security dashboard with threat analytics, investigation tool, and security health recommendations

smime.label: Available
smime.detail: S/MIME email encryption for end-to-end encrypted email with external parties

recommendations: Deploy DLP rules to auto-detect and protect sensitive data (SSNs, financial data, health records)
Configure auto-labeling via DLP to classify files based on content — reduces reliance on manual labeling
Set up trust rules to enforce per-department sharing boundaries (e.g., HR cannot share externally)
Enable context-aware access policies for T4 data — restrict to managed devices and corporate networks
Use admin-enforced IRM to automatically restrict download/print/copy on files matching DLP rules
Configure S/MIME for staff who regularly send T4 data externally (legal, HR, finance)
Use Security Center to monitor sharing activity, detect anomalies, and investigate incidents
Set up Vault retention policies aligned with your data retention schedule
Create DLP incident reports for compliance monitoring and quarterly reviews
Use security investigation tool to trace data exposure when incidents occur

free.path: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Drive > Sharing settings
free.detail: Set default to 'Only people in your organization can access files' to prevent accidental external sharing

free.path: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > User settings
free.detail: Ensure confidential mode is enabled for all users; train staff on when to use it

free.step: Set up Shared Drives with team access
free.detail: Create drives per department/function (HR, Finance, Programs, etc.). Note: per-drive sharing controls are limited on free tier
Enforce for all users with a grace period for setup. Use security keys for admins if possible.
Enable basic MDM; require screen lock; enable remote wipe capability

free.path: Admin Console > Security > API Controls > App access control
free.detail: Block or restrict untrusted third-party apps from accessing organizational data

free.step: Train staff on manual file protections
free.detail: Show staff how to restrict download/print/copy on sensitive files via Share > Settings (gear icon)

free.step: Create a data classification reference document
free.detail: Since Drive labels aren't available, create a shared document explaining your tiers and naming conventions (e.g., prefix filenames with [CONFIDENTIAL])

free.path: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > Password management
free.detail: Set minimum length to 12+ characters; enable password strength enforcement
Ensure at least 2 people have super admin access to prevent lockout

businessStandard.detail: Complete all foundational steps first before configuring Standard-tier features

businessStandard.path: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Drive > Labels
businessStandard.detail: Create labels matching your tiers: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted. Add color coding for visual identification.

businessStandard.path: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Labels
businessStandard.detail: Create matching email classification labels so staff can mark sensitive emails

businessStandard.path: Google Drive > Shared Drive > Settings (gear icon)
businessStandard.detail: For drives containing T3/T4 data: disable sharing outside the organization, disable download/copy/print for commenters and viewers
Create audiences for departments (HR, Finance, Programs) to control sharing suggestions and defaults

businessStandard.path: Google Drive > Shared Drive > Manage members
businessStandard.detail: Review membership of each Shared Drive; remove unnecessary access; use Content Manager or Contributor roles instead of Manager where possible
Train all staff to apply appropriate classification labels when creating, uploading, or modifying files

businessStandard.path: Admin Console > Reports > Apps reports > Drive
businessStandard.detail: Schedule monthly review of external sharing activity to catch drift

businessPlus.detail: Complete all foundational and Standard-tier steps first

businessPlus.path: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Google Vault
businessPlus.detail: Activate Vault and assign Vault privileges to compliance/legal staff
Set retention periods aligned with your data retention policy: e.g., 7 years for financial records, 3 years for program data, indefinite for legal holds
Set a default retention period to prevent premature deletion of data that may be needed
Create matters for any active litigation, investigation, or regulatory inquiry; add relevant custodians
Ensure at least 2 staff members know how to search, export, and manage Vault data
Create an internal procedure for legal hold requests, data exports, and retention rule changes
Run initial searches to understand what data exists and verify retention rules are capturing expected content

enterprise.detail: Complete all foundational, Standard, and Plus-tier steps first

enterprise.path: Admin Console > Security > Data Protection > Manage rules
enterprise.detail: Create rules for common sensitive data: SSNs (XXX-XX-XXXX), credit card numbers, health record identifiers. Start in 'Audit only' mode before enforcing.

enterprise.path: Admin Console > Security > Data Protection > Manage rules
enterprise.detail: Set DLP rules to automatically apply classification labels when sensitive content is detected in Drive or Gmail

enterprise.path: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Drive > Trust rules
enterprise.detail: Configure per-OU sharing restrictions: block HR and Finance from external sharing; allow Programs to share with allowlisted partner domains only
Create access levels based on device management status, IP range, and device encryption. Apply to T4 data applications.

enterprise.path: Admin Console > Security > Data Protection > Manage rules
enterprise.detail: Create DLP rules that automatically disable download/print/copy on files detected as T3/T4

enterprise.path: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > User settings > S/MIME
enterprise.detail: Enable S/MIME and distribute certificates to staff handling T4 data externally (legal, HR, executive team)
Review security health page; address critical recommendations; set up custom alerts for unusual sharing activity

enterprise.path: Admin Console > Security > Data Protection > Manage rules
enterprise.detail: Configure DLP alerts to notify security team; establish triage process for DLP incidents; document escalation path

enterprise.step: Run DLP in audit mode first
enterprise.path: Admin Console > Security > Data Protection > Manage rules
enterprise.detail: Run all new DLP rules in audit-only mode for 2-4 weeks; review logs; tune rules to reduce false positives before enforcing
Use Security Center and Drive audit logs to review who has access to T3/T4 data stores quarterly
Create a formal process for requesting DLP exceptions with approval chain and time-limited waivers
